title: BLDP Order Book Quality and Customer Concentration
text: Ballard Power Systems order backlog of $173.5M (Q4 2024) and $112.9M (Q1 2026) provides revenue visibility and execution confidence. The 12-month orderbook reached record $98.9M in Q4 2024 and $52.8M in Q1 2026. Major OEM partnerships provide long-term customer commitment and demand stability. New Flyer agreement covers 500 engines (50 MW) through mid-2026 representing significant multi-year revenue stream. Solaris Bus & Coach partnership includes platform win with ongoing order flow extending through 2026. Wrightbus relationship supports UK bus market with firm orders and options. These platform partnerships reduce customer concentration risk and provide stable recurring revenue base. The order book composition shifted toward bus segment reflecting market maturity and customer execution timelines. Rail and stationary segments show smaller order book relative to bus, reflecting earlier commercialization stages. The company expects back-half weighted revenue in 2026 reflecting timing of major customer deliveries. Order intake of $113M in 2024 and continued customer wins in 2025-2026 demonstrate ongoing demand and market acceptance. Order book quality remains strong with reputable OEMs, long-term contracts, and stable execution timelines.
